response:
The Cold War was a period of geopolitical tension between the United States and the Soviet Union, with various nations playing roles in shaping the global landscape. During this time, the United States was led by President John F. Kennedy, who initiated the Bay of Pigs invasion in 1961. The Soviet Union was led by Nikita Khrushchev, who sought to expand the influence of the communist bloc. Other countries, such as China, France, and the United Kingdom, also had their own interests and policies during this era. The Cold War ended with the fall of the Berlin Wall in 1989 and the dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991.
